step.title: Per-BC application layer goals
step.rule: Per-BC domain goals MUST exist before this step runs. Each application goal depends_on its own BC's domain goal (G-04).
One goal per BC, phase=application. Never batch multiple BCs into one goal.
All inputs are read from docs/architecture/* files and templates on disk — never from conversation context (G-16).
description max 120 chars — detail belongs in acceptance/validate arrays.

substep.title: Resolve service-contracts template
substep.action: Attempt to read the language-specific overlay template first, then fall back to base:
Store the resolved template content as SERVICE_CONTRACTS_TEMPLATE. Record which tier was used.
substep.rule: Handler injection patterns and read model interface conventions MUST come from the resolved template, not hardcoded (G-16).

substep.title: Read architecture inputs
substep.action: Read the following files and extract structured data for the application layer loop:

read.extract: All level-3 headings under "## Bounded Context Inventory" — the BC names.
For each BC, the comma-separated aggregate names from "- **Aggregates:** " field.

For each BC, partition endpoints into: - COMMANDS: POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE methods → command handlers (mutations) - QUERIES: GET methods → query handlers (reads) Store as USE_CASE_MAP[bc_name] = { commands: [...], queries: [...] }
GET endpoints with collection paths (no trailing ID segment) or descriptions containing "list"/"search"/"index". Tag each with BC name for read model interface detection.
For each BC and aggregate, extract the repository interface name pattern: {Aggregate}RepositoryInterface. Also check "Read model interface (query):" fields for read model interface names.

substep.action: For each BC, compute HANDLER_COUNT = len(commands) + len(queries).
For each BC with LIST_ACTIONS, note that ReadModelInterface + DTO + Criteria files are needed.

substep.title: Determine domain goal IDs for depends_on
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Filter all goal entries where phase=domain. Map each domain goal to its BC name (from the goal description or context).
For each BC in BC_LIST, find its corresponding domain goal ID. Store as APP_DEPENDS_ON[bc_name] = domain_goal_id.

check.condition: any BC in BC_LIST has no matching domain goal
check.action: ABORT with error: "BC '{{bc_name}}' has no domain goal in goals.yaml — run domain goal generation first"

substep.rule: Each application goal depends_on ONLY its own BC's domain goal — not other BCs' goals (G-04).
Domain goal IDs MUST be dynamically read from goals.yaml, never hardcoded or computed from position.

substep.title: Loop: compose and create application goals per BC
substep.action: For each BC in BC_LIST, execute substeps 3.15.4a through 3.15.4f:

substep.title: Compose acceptance criteria (PA-01..PA-12)
substep.action: Build ACCEPTANCE array with all 12 application layer criteria from docs/task-plan-spec.md:564-577, parameterized with BC name and handler count:

criteria.item: Each use case from api-endpoints.md has exactly one handler class ({{HANDLER_COUNT}} handlers for {{BC_NAME}})
Handlers inject repository interfaces (ports), not implementations — constructor params use {{Aggregate}}RepositoryInterface type hints
Zero imports from Infrastructure namespace in src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ files
Zero imports from HTTP/Controller namespace in src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ files
Command DTOs have no business logic — only public properties or constructor, no methods with domain operations
Command handlers __invoke() returns void or scalar ID type
Query handlers __invoke() returns DTO objects, never entities or aggregate roots
Unit tests mock repository/read-model interfaces, not Doctrine implementations
Unit tests cover: happy path, validation failure, domain exception propagation per handler
vendor/bin/phpstan analyse src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ --level=9 passes (exit 0, zero errors)
vendor/bin/deptrac analyse --filter={{BC_NAME}} passes — Application depends only on Domain + Share

substep.rule: All 12 criteria must be present as individual strings in the acceptance array — no omissions, no merging.

substep.title: Compose validation commands
substep.action: Build VALIDATE array with 3 CLI commands scoped to this BC's Application namespace:
substep.rule: Validation commands are BC-scoped — not project-wide. Each command verifies one of PA-10, PA-11, PA-12.

substep.title: Compose context with Investigation Config and fan-out hints
substep.action: Build CONTEXT string with these sections:
substep.section: Application layer for {{BC_NAME}}: command/query handlers orchestrate domain operations via ports. Use cases from docs/architecture/api-endpoints.md ({{BC_NAME}} section). Handler injection patterns from {{SERVICE_CONTRACTS_TEMPLATE_TIER}} template (service-contracts.md). Domain interfaces (ports) defined in own BC's domain goal deliverables. Command handlers: receive Command DTO, invoke aggregate via repository interface, return void/scalar ID. Query handlers: receive Query DTO, invoke read model interface, return DTO (never entity/aggregate). Application layer does NOT dispatch domain events — that is infrastructure's flush-then-dispatch responsibility.

section.name: deliverables
section.action: List concrete file paths per handler based on USE_CASE_MAP for this BC:
section.template:
For each command use case: - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Command/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Command.php (DTO) - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Command/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Handler.php - tests/Unit/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Command/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}HandlerTest.php
For each query use case: - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Query/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Query.php (DTO) - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Query/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}Handler.php - tests/Unit/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/Query/{{UseCaseName}}/{{UseCaseName}}HandlerTest.php
If BC has list/search use cases: - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ReadModel/{{ReadModel}}ReadModelInterface.php - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ReadModel/{{ReadModel}}DTO.php - src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/ReadModel/{{ReadModel}}Criteria.php

section.name: fan-out-hints
section.hint: Single-task goal per BC. Handlers within one BC share the same aggregate ports — splitting would force duplicated mocking setup with no isolation benefit. Multi-task only if BC has >6 handlers.

section.name: investigation-config

investigator.name: Unit tests
investigator.pass: Exit 0 AND run is non-empty (test count present in output, NOT "No tests executed!"), all tests green — happy path, validation failure, and domain exception cases covered
investigator.fail: Test failures, errors, or missing coverage for required scenarios

investigator.name: PHPStan
investigator.pass: Exit 0, zero errors — all type hints resolve, interface contracts satisfied
investigator.fail: Type errors, missing return types, or unresolved interface references

investigator.name: Deptrac
investigator.pass: Exit 0 — Application layer depends only on Domain + Share, zero Infrastructure/Controller imports
investigator.fail: Dependency violation: Application importing from Infrastructure or Controller namespace

section.rule: Exactly 3 investigators per application goal (GM-08). Each has type, commands, pass/fail criteria (GM-09).

substep.title: Compose not-in-scope
substep.value: Doctrine mappings, database migrations, HTTP controllers, routes, domain logic, event dispatching, infrastructure implementations, Symfony service configuration — all handled by domain or infrastructure goals.

substep.title: Inject code rules (apply [CODE-RULE-INJECTION])
substep.action: Bind RULE_FILES = the comma-joined deliverable paths for this BC's application layer (substep 3.15.4c deliverables section — the Command/Query/ReadModel handler+DTO paths). Bind RULE_PHASE = `application` (this goal's phase token — pass it VERBATIM).

Run `tmux-cli rules match --files <RULE_FILES> --phase <RULE_PHASE> --json` and apply the spine's `[CODE-RULE-INJECTION]` procedure (<conventions> / <rule-to-goal-injection>): copy each NON-EMPTY `acceptance_line` into the ACCEPTANCE array (3.15.4a); copy each `automated`/`mixed` `validate_cmd` into the VALIDATE array (3.15.4b); add the `Code rules: <ids> — read <paths>` line to CONTEXT (3.15.4c) and to the goal.md written in 3.15.4f. Empty `rules` ⇒ inject nothing.

If `review` `validate_cmd`s exist, ADD ONE convention-audit investigator carrying ALL of them (3.15.4c emits exactly 3 base investigators: test-execution / static-analysis / architecture-check, so the ADD lands the 4th slot — legal, no drop). If none, emit no investigator.
substep.rule: Copy pre-rendered payload fields VERBATIM — no agent-side glob/severity logic. PRESERVE the 3 existing investigators; the convention-audit is additive only.

substep.title: Call goal-create MCP
substep.action: Call the goal-create MCP tool with these parameters:

params.param: Implement {{BC_NAME}} application layer: command/query handlers and DTOs
ACCEPTANCE array from substep 3.15.4a (exactly 12 items, PA-01..PA-12)
CONTEXT string from substep 3.15.4c (purpose + deliverables + fan-out hints)

Array of {name,type,paths,commands,pass,fail,condition?} objects built from substep 3.15.4c's investigators (2-4; types from the Reference Table). Omit conditional investigators whose condition is false.
APP_DEPENDS_ON[bc_name] — own BC's domain goal ID from goals.yaml

SCOPE array — see the <scope-derivation> rule in the spine <conventions> block. Derive from this BC's application Deliverables (substep 3.15.4c): emit ["src/{{BC_NAME}}/Application/**"] (plus any extra distinct deliverable dirs). Single-BC application namespace ⇒ disjoint from other BCs ⇒ co-schedulable under MaxGoals>1.

substep.rule: Description pattern "Implement {BC} application layer: command/query handlers and DTOs" stays under 120 chars for BC names up to ~40 chars.
max_retries=3 — application goals have focused scope (single BC namespace).

check.action: Print error with full context. Continue to next BC — do not abort the entire loop for a single BC failure.
Print: "Application goal created for {{BC_NAME}}: goal-{{ID}} (phase=application, depends_on=[{{DOMAIN_GOAL_ID}}]) with 3 investigators, {{HANDLER_COUNT}} handlers, max_retries=3"

substep.title: Write goal.md
substep.action: Write a goal.md file in the goal directory with these sections:
substep.template:
## Description Implement {{BC_NAME}} application layer: command/query handlers for {{HANDLER_COUNT}} use cases. Command handlers receive DTOs, orchestrate domain via repository interfaces, return void/scalar. Query handlers receive query DTOs, invoke read model interfaces, return DTOs.
## Dependencies - {{DOMAIN_GOAL_ID}}: {{BC_NAME}} domain layer (provides aggregate interfaces and value objects)
## Deliverables (File paths from substep 3.15.4c deliverables section)
## Acceptance Criteria PA-01 through PA-12 (from substep 3.15.4a)
## Fan-out Hints Single-task goal. All handlers in {{BC_NAME}} share the same aggregate ports — splitting would duplicate mocking setup with no isolation benefit. Multi-task only if handler count > 6.
## Preconditions (Declarative env/service gates the daemon evaluates before dispatching this goal, and re-evaluates on the §5 auto-resume loop. One line each: "- [env] VAR_NAME — remedy" or "- [service] host:port — remedy". Write "none" if the goal has no external precondition.) none
## Not In Scope Doctrine mappings, database migrations, HTTP controllers, routes, domain logic, event dispatching, infrastructure implementations, Symfony service configuration.

substep.title: Report completion
substep.action: After all BCs are processed, print summary:
